title: feat: implement volume rename functionality
body:
## Description
This pull request introduces support for renaming volumes in Podman, including CLI, API, and backend/database changes. The new feature allows users to rename existing volumes with various safety checks and restrictions, and is fully documented and exposed via the CLI, REST API, and Go bindings.

### Feature: Volume Rename Support
Adds a new podman volume rename command to the CLI, allowing users to rename volumes, with proper validation and error handling.
Documents the new command in podman-volume-rename.1.md and updates the main volume command documentation to include the rename subcommand.

### Backend and API Implementation
Implements RenameVolume in the runtime, enforcing restrictions (e.g., volume not in use, only local driver, not anonymous, etc.), renaming the filesystem directory, updating the database, and emitting an event.
Adds a RenameVolume method to the database state interface and provides an implementation for SQLite, ensuring atomic updates to both the volume's config and state.
Exposes the rename operation via the REST API (/libpod/volumes/{name}/rename), including handler, route registration, and error handling.

#### Checklist

- [x] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [x] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [x] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [x] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
Add `podman volume rename` command to rename existing volumes in-place. Only volumes using the local driver that are not in use by any containers can be renamed.
```

Fixes #28189
